ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-21-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Status on filing of a petition for preliminary distribution is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution is set for October 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An order to show cause re sanctions is also being issued for October 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Attorney Kathryn Korn to appear and show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear pursuant to CCP 128.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-01-2023 Continued To Jun-05-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Korn appeared by zoom for Alan Lindsley (executor).

The court stated that there was a question about how to calculate the statutory compensation, but it thinks we are in agreement on the figure at this point. The court also asked Mrs. Korn why she obtained/had a second set of letters issued. Mrs. Korn stated that she thinks a financial institution wanted recent letters, and that she thinks they may have a stale check floating around. Mrs. Korn also stated that she spoke with Mr. Newberry last week, that it appears they have an agreement, that Mr. Newberry will be paying $2,500.00 a month directly to the University of the Pacific, that she does not have a written signed agreement, and suggested a hold back in the amount of $5,000.00 to keep the probate open. Discussion was also held with regard to doing a preliminary distribution to the University of the Pacific, the issue of only being able to do a preliminary distribution for half of what's in the estate, what an appropriate amount would be, the amount of funds that are in the estate, the current petition being framed in a manner which does not allow the court to approve a preliminary distribution, what an appropriate amount of a reserve would be, the accounting petition going off calendar, etc. The First and Final Account and Report of Executor, etc. is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for preliminary distribution is set for May 1, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody.. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Continued To Sep-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Korn appeared by zoom for with Alan Lindlsey (Petitioner) by zoom.
Robert Berryman (alternate Executor) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that additional information has been provided, and that it has been reviewed, but the statements/letters are not verified/not signed under penalty of perjury. Ms. Korn stated that supplemental declaration is verified, and that it mentions that her Client was able to go to the Decedent's home and search for the will. Mr. Lindsley that he and Bob searched the home a number of times and the safe deposit box, and that they did not find the current will, but they found the old wills. The Court stated that it is satisfied with the will, that the presumption of revocation has been met and that the lost will accurately reflects the Decedent's intent. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 11, 2016 is admitted to probate. Alan Lindsley is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for August 23, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed one week before the hearing, the status hearing will be taken off calendar. If not, Ms. Korn will need to appear on that date to explain why nothing has been filed. Ms. Korn advised the Court that there is an issue of elder abuse and that there will be a need for a reserve.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Korn appeared by zoom for Alan Lindsey (Petitioner).

The Court referred to Probate Code 6124 and stated that the code allows someone to petition for admission of a lost will, but when the will is lost while in the possession of the Testator, there is an evidentiary presumption that the will was revoked and that it needs some affirmative evidence to overcome the presumption of revocation such as a declaration from the estate planning Attorney. Ms. Korn stated that she spoke with Bill Sherer (estate planning attorney) and that it looks like he had drafted a prior will and it was re-written to change the Executors. Ms. Korn also stated that the Decedent's close friends and neighbors confirmed that they believe this is the last will and the Decedent did not indicate that he intended to revoke the will, and that they were very close to the Decedent. Ms. Korn also explained that Mr. Bateman went to the home of the Decedent to do a search because her Client was ill, that the home is very, very cluttered, that Mr. Bateman found a sealed envelope in the office in a file folder that said "my will" and referred to attachment 3f(3) of the supplement that was filed on December 7, 2020. The Court suggested getting declarations from the neighbors and Mr. Bateman, addressed the issue of the omitted heirs stating that it is satisfied with the due diligence regarding the data base searches, and asked about searching the Decedent's office for address books, or getting declarations from the close friends. Ms. Korn stated that Mr. Lindsley did speak with the neighbors, that the Decedent was estranged from his family and assured the Court that Mr. Lindsley will be able to serve despite his illness. Ms. Korn also stated that the will was typed out on a computer and that the computer dated it May 10, 2016, but that it was May 11, 2016 when they all signed it. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to February 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any new submissions are to be filed by February 17, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
